page.text:
Dangerous goods
When handling dangerous goods you need to find out the IMO classification of the goods. If the goods are flammable or poisonous, you need to handle them with caution. If goods emit flammable gases in contact with water, you need to keep them dry. If goods are liable to spontaneous heating and combustion, do not touch them.
You always need to follow the instructions on compatibility and the stowage of dangerous goods. Find out the IMDG-Code when loading and stowing. Check the proper segregation of goods and the correct technical names in documents. Check correct marks and labels and find out the compatibility of the IMO Class of the goods and stow them correctly.

Different labels are used to indicate how to store different dangerous goods. Orange labels indicate that dangerous goods must be separated from flammable liquids. Red labels indicate that dangerous goods must be separated from flammable solids, spontaneously combustible agents or agents that are dangerous when wet. Green labels indicate that dangerous goods must be stored away from explosive goods. Yellow labels indicate that dangerous goods cannot be stored near flammable gas, non-flammable compressed gas or toxic gas.
